content.claim: Geographic distribution of 6/8-year gimnaziums is heavily urban-skewed: approximately 48% in small towns, 25% in provincial county seats, 23% in Budapest, and only 3% in villages; this settlement concentration insulates elite tracks from rural depopulation while ordinary schools absorb enrollment decline.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: Fieldwork cited but no primary source document provided
Over-application rates by settlement type are mentioned qualitatively but not quantified
Does not specify whether urban in-migration will sustain enrollment in elite urban tracks during demographic decline
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
